headline: CCJ oversold. CCJ and NVDA cooling below MA50. Entry window open.
marketContext: Cycle is in Transition / verification (confidence 83%) — posture is watch_only. Fundamentals are running ahead of price in AI Infrastructure Hardware and Nuclear / SMR — Signal vs. Speculation and Defense Tech / Unmanned Systems — the market's fear is the opportunity. Broad Market — Late Bull Extension is priced ahead of fundamentals — don't chase.
groupAContext: CLS (RSI 45.4, -26.1% from peak), KTOS (RSI 48.2, -60% from peak), BWXT (RSI 48.9, -18.6% from peak) are cooling — early-entry thesis intact. GEV and KNSL are extended (RSI 60.5/65.7) — no new entry here, let momentum resolve. Best setup: CLS at RSI 45.4 — -26.1% from 52-week high with narrative not yet priced by institutions.
groupBContext: CCJ (RSI 38.5) is technically oversold — historically a high-quality entry signal for moat names. NVDA (RSI 42, -7% vs MA50), PWR (RSI 44, -5% vs MA50), AVGO (RSI 44.7, -9% vs MA50) are below MA50 with cooling RSI — approaching entry range. Highest conviction: AVGO (score 81) — use RSI and MA50 levels to time entry, not to validate the thesis.

topEntry.name: Broadcom
topEntry.why: RSI 44.7 (cooling), 8.5% below MA50, -24.5% from 52-week high. Conviction score 81. Deep hyperscaler relationships. Custom ASIC design is irreplaceable once embedded — hyperscalers design multi-year chip …
